mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.5 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and falling federal funds rates amplifies consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns (Rank IC consistently near zero) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding a 0.4 percent threshold triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized labor and housing market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and HOUST yields a negative predictive accuracy for 60-day returns, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month confirms resilient consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth above 1.0% MoM fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with a directional accuracy below 40%.
